Title: The CsWRKY50-CsREM1-CsTSI module inhibits theanine biosynthesis in tea plants under drought stress

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Leaf; expression decreases with increasing drought stress duration
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Leaf under drought stress; strongly positively correlated with theanine content
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes the biosynthesis of theanine in tea plants; identified as the key theanine synthase associated with leaf theanine accumulation under drought stress
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major contributor to umami flavor and tea quality
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key control point/key enzyme for theanine biosynthesis in tea leaves under drought stress
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher CsTSI expression is associated with increased theanine accumulation; drought-induced downregulation of CsTSI reduces theanine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; HPLC; Y1H and dual-luciferase/EMSA evidence through upstream TFs; VIGS and transient overexpression of regulators
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: CsREM1; CsWRKY50 (indirect upstream regulator via CsREM1)
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: ProCsTSI (native promoter in reporter assays); 35S (used for upstream TF effector constructs in transient assays)
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq drought transcriptome (Illumina NovaSeq 6000); WGCNA based on transcriptome; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target to maintain or enhance theanine accumulation in tea leaves under drought, potentially by promoter engineering or stacking with positive regulator CsREM1 and/or reducing CsWRKY50 activity
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsTSI expression showed the strongest positive correlation with theanine content among examined theanine-pathway genes under drought stress. 2. Drought stress progressively reduced CsTSI transcript abundance in leaves. 3. CsREM1 directly activated CsTSI transcription, while drought-induced CsWRKY50 indirectly suppressed CsTSI via repression of CsREM1.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified as the principal theanine synthase in this study relative to other CsTS genes.

Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Leaf; decreases under prolonged drought stress; positively correlated with theanine content and CsTSI expression
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Leaf; reduced by drought stress; increased in overexpression assays
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major contributor to umami flavor and tea quality
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Directly activates CsTSI transcription by binding its promoter
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Drought stress negatively affects CsREM1 expression; CsWRKY50 acts upstream as a transcriptional repressor
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: CsTSI promoter; CsWRKY50 (upstream regulator of CsREM1 promoter)
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes theanine biosynthesis by upregulating CsTSI; silencing reduces theanine and overexpression increases theanine
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential positive regulator linking transcriptional activation of CsTSI to elevated theanine accumulation in leaves
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y1H; dual-luciferase assay; EMSA; VIGS silencing; transient overexpression; RT-qPCR; HPLC
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S::CsREM1 for effector/overexpression; ProCsTSI::LUC in reporter assays
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq drought transcriptome; Y1H library screening; accession NA
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate positive regulator for engineering high-theanine tea, potentially via overexpression or promoter editing to escape drought-induced repression
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsREM1 was identified by Y1H screening as a TF that binds the CsTSI promoter. 2. CsREM1 localizes to the nucleus and binds TATGTAG and CTACACA motifs in the CsTSI promoter. 3. CsREM1 activates CsTSI transcription and promotes theanine accumulation, as shown by dual-luciferase, VIGS, transient overexpression, RT-qPCR, and HPLC.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Named based on phylogenetic clustering with Arabidopsis AtREM1; Positive Clone 10 from the Y1H screen.

Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Leaf; strongly upregulated as drought stress duration increases
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Leaf under drought stress; elevated in overexpression lines and reduced in VIGS lines
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Major contributor to umami flavor and tea quality
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Transcriptional repression of positive regulatory pathway controlling theanine biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Directly represses CsREM1 transcription; indirectly downregulates CsTSI
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Inhibits theanine biosynthesis; silencing increases theanine and overexpression decreases theanine
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Drought-induced negative regulator that suppresses the CsREM1-CsTSI positive module and reduces leaf theanine accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: WGCNA; RT-qPCR; Y1H; dual-luciferase assay; EMSA; VIGS silencing; transient overexpression; HPLC
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: PEG-6000-simulated drought stress; additional 24 h 10% PEG-6000 treatment after VIGS
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S::CsWRKY50 for effector/overexpression; ProCsREM1::LUC in reporter assays
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq drought transcriptome; WGCNA on drought transcriptome; accession NA
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value target for suppressing drought-related theanine loss, for example by gene editing, knockdown, or disrupting W-box-mediated repression of CsREM1
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. WGCNA identified CsWRKY50 as a drought-responsive hub TF associated with theanine, CsREM1, and CsTSI. 2. CsWRKY50 localizes to the nucleus and binds the W-box (TGACT) motif in the CsREM1 promoter. 3. CsWRKY50 represses CsREM1 transcription, thereby lowering CsTSI expression and reducing theanine accumulation under drought stress.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Named based on phylogenetic clustering with Arabidopsis AtWRKY50; acts upstream of CsREM1 in a drought-responsive transcriptional cascade.
